widget_icons.overview: This file documents the automatic widget icon support in the BAW toolkit packager. Icons provide visual identification for widgets in the IBM Business Automation Workflow Process Designer palette, improving user experience and widget discoverability.

file_format.rationale: SVG format ensures crisp display at any size and keeps file sizes small

file_naming.rule: Icon file must be named exactly as {WidgetName}.svg
file_naming.case_sensitive: Yes - must match the widget name exactly including capitalization

file_location.location: Widget root directory (same level as widget/ subdirectory)
file_location.structure: widgets/ ProgressBar/ ProgressBar.svg ← Icon file here README.md widget/ config.json Layout.html inlineJavascript.js InlineCSS.css

recommended_dimensions.rationale: Optimal size for BAW palette display while maintaining clarity

design_guidelines.guideline: Use simple, recognizable shapes that represent the widget's purpose
Maintain good contrast for visibility in both light and dark themes
Keep file size under 10KB for fast loading
Avoid excessive detail that may not be visible at small sizes
Use consistent color palette across related widgets

step.action: Widget scanner checks for {WidgetName}.svg in widget root directory
If icon exists, generate unique managed asset ID from registry
Package icon as managed asset (type 61) with MIME type image/svg+xml
Link icon to coach view via paletteIcon element
Register icon ID in registry for stable versioning

automatic_packaging.no_configuration_needed: Icon detection and packaging is completely automatic. No changes to configuration files or packaging scripts are required. Simply place the correctly named SVG file in the widget directory and it will be included in the next package build.

registry_tracking.description: Icon IDs are tracked in toolkit_packager/baw_coach_views.json registry to maintain stable IDs across packaging operations. This ensures proper version control and prevents ID conflicts when updating toolkits.

twx_package_structure.description: Icons are packaged as managed assets in the TWX file structure
twx_package_structure.structure: Custom_Widgets_1.0.28.twx ├── META-INF/ │ ├── package.xml ← Lists icon as managed asset object │ └── metadata.xml ├── objects/ │ ├── 64.xxx.xml ← Coach view with paletteIcon reference │ └── 61.yyy.xml ← Icon managed asset definition └── files/ └── 61.yyy/ └── {hash} ← Actual SVG file content

coach_view_reference.description: Icons are linked in coach view XML via paletteIcon element

managed_asset_definition.description: Icon managed asset XML defines the SVG file

issue.name: icon_not_appearing
issue.symptom: Widget packages successfully but icon doesn't appear in BAW palette

possible_causes.cause: Icon file name doesn't exactly match widget name (case-sensitive)
Icon file is not in widget root directory
Icon file is not valid SVG format
Previous toolkit version is cached in BAW

diagnostic_steps.step: Check file name matches widget name exactly (case-sensitive)
Validate SVG file opens correctly in a browser or SVG viewer
Extract TWX package and verify icon is included in objects/ directory
Check coach view XML contains paletteIcon reference (not isNull="true")
Clear BAW cache or increment toolkit version number

issue.name: icon_id_conflicts
issue.symptom: Packaging fails with icon ID conflicts or duplicate IDs

possible_causes.cause: Registry file (baw_coach_views.json) is corrupted or out of sync
Manual edits to registry file introduced invalid IDs

resolution.step: Remove the iconId entry for the affected widget
Re-run packaging to generate fresh icon ID
If problem persists, regenerate entire registry from scratch

issue.name: icon_file_too_large
issue.symptom: TWX package size is unexpectedly large

possible_causes.cause: SVG file contains embedded raster images
SVG has excessive detail or unoptimized paths

resolution.step: Optimize SVG using tools like SVGO or Inkscape
Remove embedded images and use pure vector graphics
Simplify complex paths and reduce number of nodes
Target file size under 10KB per icon

before_packaging.item: Icon file exists in widget root directory
Icon filename exactly matches widget name (case-sensitive)
Icon file size is reasonable (under 10KB recommended)

after_packaging.item: Check packaging log confirms icon was detected and packaged
Verify TWX package contains icon managed asset (61.xxx.xml)
Confirm coach view XML has paletteIcon reference (not isNull)
Extract and inspect actual SVG file content in TWX package

after_deployment.item: Open BAW Process Designer and check widget palette
Verify icon appears next to widget name
Confirm icon displays clearly at palette size
Test icon visibility in both light and dark themes (if applicable)

practice.name: Create icons for all widgets
practice.description: While icons are optional, they significantly improve user experience by making widgets easier to identify and distinguish in the palette.

practice.name: Use consistent design language
practice.description: Maintain visual consistency across all widget icons in your toolkit. Use similar styles, colors, and design patterns to create a cohesive set.

practice.name: Test icons at actual size
practice.description: View icons at the size they'll appear in the BAW palette (typically 24-32px) to ensure they remain clear and recognizable when scaled down.

practice.name: Keep SVG code clean
practice.description: Use optimized SVG code without unnecessary metadata, comments, or hidden elements. This reduces file size and improves loading performance.

practice.name: Version control icon files
practice.description: Track icon files in version control alongside widget code. This maintains history and allows reverting to previous icon designs if needed.

example_workflow.scenario: Adding an icon to an existing widget

step.action: Create or obtain SVG icon file (120x120px recommended)
step.command: Design icon using vector graphics tool or download from icon library

step.action: Name icon file to match widget name exactly
Verify icon file is valid SVG
step.command: Open ProgressBar.svg in browser to confirm it displays correctly

step.expected_output: INFO: Reusing existing icon ID for 'ProgressBar': 61.19d8bd43-d600-41db-a9ca-5a6ee876a762 (or) INFO: Generated new icon ID for 'ProgressBar': 61.19d8bd43-d600-41db-a9ca-5a6ee876a762
Shows icon managed asset file in package

step.action: Deploy to BAW and verify icon appears in palette
step.command: Use MCP deployment or manual import

component.name: Widget Model
component.description: Provides icon detection and content retrieval for widgets

component.name: Coach View Generator
component.description: Generates coach view XML with paletteIcon reference

component.name: Managed Asset Generator
component.description: Creates managed asset objects for icon SVG files

component.name: Coach View Registry
component.description: Tracks icon IDs for stable versioning across builds
